title: Pyrrosia lauterbachii
taxonomy: Pyrrosia lauterbachii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrrosia, which includes various species of tropical ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is a large group of vascular plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. This family includes over 1,200 species of ferns and is one of the largest families of flowering plants. Pyrrosia lauterbachii is likely to be classified as a member of this family due to its characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Pyrrosia lauterbachii, also known as the New Guinea shield fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Pyrrosia lauterbachii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with other medications and its limited scientific research.
